name: 鄭宇哲 UJ Cheng
title: Garmin Advanced Software Engineer
intro: Advanced Software Engineer at Garmin. 10 years of iOS development experience since college. Love solving the LeetCode problems with Swift.

name: 吳政賢 OAWU
title: An Ordinary Web Engineer
intro:
I am a software developer who loves programming, with no particular preference for any specific domain. The key is to solve problems and achieve goals, regardless of the means.
People often ask whether I prefer frontend, backend, App, or system development? Honestly, I'm not sure. For me, the source of satisfaction comes from developing products that "people use and love."
I don't chase new technologies much; compared to technology, I care more about whether my work is appreciated.
Due to my passion for temple festivals, I've worked my way from backend, frontend, and systems all the way to App development, creating a service that has received positive feedback from over 50,000 people.

name: zonble
title: Flutter GDE. Software Engineer.
intro: zonble has years of Apple platform development experience, authored "KKBOX iOS/Mac OS X Basic Development Materials," and has been actively sharing practical insights at CocoaHeads Taipei and various technical conferences.

name: Yuki Aki
title: LY Corporation iOS Engineer
intro: Yuki is an iOS Engineer at LY Corporation. He works for LINE, improving the Build System of the LINE application (which is most popular messaging application in Japan). He has spoken at many conferences and meetups as he enjoys talking about what he learned while coding in Swift. He also contributed to OSS projects, like Swift Compiler, fastlane, and XcodeGen.

name: 東東
title: iOS Intern @LINE & Tech Lead @OpenNCCU
intro:
Self-proclaimed interdisciplinary engineer.
Business card says iOS engineer, but skill tree is maxed out in React and Vue.
Sometimes can't resist the urge to write some Go and Rust.
Hopes to add a bit of freshness to the world through my own products.

name: 林子祐
title:
University of Washington Student
Taiwan EEW App Owner / Independent Developer
intro: I'm Lin Zih-You, currently a sophomore in the College of Engineering at the University of Washington, preparing to develop in the direction of computer engineering. I love programming and tinkering with hardware, and I really enjoy turning small ideas into real things. "Taiwan Earthquake Early Warning" was originally just a small project I worked on during my spare time for self-learning, but unexpectedly it grew to 2 million downloads across Taiwan, becoming the app everyone opens first when an earthquake hits. Along the way, I learned not only programming, but also how to respond to user feedback, how to calmly debug during midnight earthquakes, and how not to be startled awake by my own push notifications. Besides the app, I also interned at ITRI, participating in smart robot development (Jetson Nano platform), and during the semester I developed STM32 firmware in the campus Husky Robotics club. I hope through this sharing, I can tell you my development story, maybe helping you avoid some pitfalls and accomplish more of what you want to achieve.

name: qcl
title: LINE Taiwan Limited  Senior iOS Developer
intro: Previously worked at Yahoo Taiwan and Booking.com Netherlands, currently a Senior iOS Engineer at LINE Taiwan Limited. Unknowingly accumulated 10 years of experience from product feature development and user interface implementation all the way to infrastructure and architecture design, with the user scale of responsible products growing increasingly large. Besides programming, also enjoys city simulation games, with over a thousand hours of Cities: Skylines gameplay. Compared to gaming, in recent years has invested over ten thousand hours in the field of parenting.

name: Eric Chen
title: iOS Developer
intro:
Hi, I'm Eric, an iOS engineer at OpenNet and also a dad juggling part-time parenting.
I've been fortunate to participate in several architecture improvement projects, benefiting from the rich experience shared by team colleagues and the community, which allowed me to gradually figure out feasible approaches from chaotic projects.
This time I want to share in the most authentic way the processes and choices we actually went through.

name: Vincent Chen
title:
Rakuten Group, Inc.
Assistant Manager / Lead of iOS team, Rebates Section
intro: Vincent Chen currently works at Rakuten Group, Inc., leading the Rebates iOS team. He is a senior iOS developer who started with LAMP stack, with over 10 years of experience, specializing in cross-functional collaboration and exploring legacy project refactoring. He once single-handedly took over a long-unmaintained iOS project while gradually introducing modern tools and building a team. Currently residing in Japan, he loves skiing in winter and enjoys driving, knitting, and cooking Taiwanese cuisine at home. Recently, he has been actively exploring the practicality of various AI tools in existing projects.

name: YC Hsu
title: vocus Mobile Product Lead
intro: Mobile Product Lead at vocus; practitioner of TCA; passionate about crafting delightful and impactful products for the creator economy. ✨

name: Art
title: Ubiquiti Inc. iOS Team Lead
intro: Since witnessing the birth of SwiftUI firsthand at WWDC 2019, Art has been passionate about researching SwiftUI-related technologies. After joining Ubiquiti in 2022, he has been continuously developing with SwiftUI + TCA to this day. Currently, due to stepping one foot into management, he exists in a superposition state of being able to write code and not being able to write code.

name: 高海峰 Hai Feng Kao
intro: Regular speaker at iOS@Taipei meetups

name: Allen Lai
title: KKCompany(KKBOX) Senior Engineer
intro: Became an Apple ecosystem developer starting from iOS 9. Majored in multimedia design and biological image recognition in college. First job was as a PM at a software contracting company, responsible for everything except coding. Has worked at multiple companies and faced numerous interview challenges. Currently works as a senior iOS engineer at KKCompany(KKBOX) and has a lovely daughter :)

name: Ben
title: Founder of Laughing Cat Curry
intro: iOS Developer, co-founder of Laughing Cat Curry. The best place in the world for coding must have snow.

name: Harry 吳卓強
title: Build with Harry Instructor
intro: Harry is an experienced iOS engineer, educator, and entrepreneur with over 13 years of professional experience in mobile application development. He teaches iOS and AI-assisted application development at universities and conferences, inspiring the next generation of developers.

name: 海總理
title: USPACE AI Strategy & Application Director / CEO of Ocean Limited
intro:
USPACE AI Director
Former General Manager of StreetVoice

Passionate about developing interesting and useful products, enjoys using AI to solve previously unsolvable problems, and leveraging AI and automation to improve overall company operational efficiency.

name: 大軍
title: iOS Developer
intro: Started iOS development in 2016, also served as Design Director for iPlayground and has given talks. Recently fascinated by AI-related technologies and hopes to discuss and share with friends who are researching related technologies.

name: Daiki Matsudate
intro:
Daiki is an iOS engineer from Tokyo and the organizer of try! Swift Tokyo—basically, he throws big Swift parties and calls them conferences. With 14+ years of app-building experience, he now works on projects ranging from startups to public digital services.
When he’s not working, you’ll find him playing games, studying wine, or chasing the beauty of Japan’s changing seasons.

name: 彼得潘
title: Swift iOS & Flutter App Development Author and Instructor
intro: Super passionate about Apple, engaged in various Apple-related work. Developed some iOS Apps, wrote some Swift programming books and articles, taught some SwiftUI / UIKit / Flutter App development courses, and met many friends who also love developing Apps.

name: 羊小咩
title: Chief Information Officer at O'Pay Electronic Payment
intro: Currently serves as CIO of O'Pay Electronic Payment, holding PMP (Project Management Professional), CSM (Certified ScrumMaster), and CLB (Certified Large-scale agile) certifications. Has experience building multiple large-scale electronic payment systems and financial information security. Serves as a committee member of F-ISAC (Financial Information Sharing and Analysis Center) and the Financial Information Security Review Committee, overseeing Taiwan's payment operators and security regulation formulation and promotion. Won excellence award in the 12th iThome Ironman Contest cybersecurity category. Co-published 'App Information Security That Not Only Engineers Need to Understand' with Gotop Publishing, dedicated to promoting financial payment security.

name: Astrid Lin
title: iOS developer
intro: Astrid is an iOS developer at KKBOX, where she focuses on creating exceptional user experiences for music lovers. She's interested in user experience and CI/CD practices. Inspired by her first tech conference in Japan, Astrid is passionate about contributing to the developer community. Based in Taipei, she enjoys film and reading in her free time, and loves to travel.

name: Ethan
title: 9count General Manager
intro: Ethan Fan currently serves as General Manager of Asia at 9count. Before joining 9count, he co-founded two Silicon Valley startups and was selected for Stanford's startup accelerator StartX and Techstars Toronto. The technologies and mobile applications developed by these two startups were featured in Google I/O keynotes, TC Disrupt Battlefield, and Apple Watch App Store. He also holds over 10 US patents.

name: 倪僑德 Aaron Ni
title: PicCollage iOS Developer
intro:
Aaron Ni is one of the regular speakers at iOS@Taipei.
We hold various iOS topic sharing sessions online on the first Tuesday of every month. Welcome to join us if you're interested!
https://www.facebook.com/groups/ios.taipei

name: 劉信
title: Independent Developer
intro: Liu Xin is an independent Apple platform developer with ten years of software development experience and an active participant in the developer community. He is passionate about the Swift language and free research and creation. He has spent his life constantly thinking about the nature and methods of learning. After obtaining his degree in related fields from Carnegie Mellon University, he positioned himself as a "Learning Engineer," continuously exploring how to use scientific thinking to improve learning-related products and experiences.

title: Apple Dev Weekly author, weak self podcast host. Welcome to subscribe 13+
intro: Love being inspired and sharing inspiration.
